data.text: I’ll execute the bead directly: first inspect the named routing files and references, then remove only the obsolete DDx routing implementation/tests, run the relevant Go tests, and commit the scoped change.

The worktree already has unrelated dirty `.agents/skills` changes, so I’m leaving those alone. The first reference scan shows the obsolete APIs are also used by a couple of DDx test files, so I’m checking whether those tests should be removed or rewritten against the upstream service API to satisfy the “no remaining references” criterion.

I’m going to make the scoped deletion now. One small supporting change is needed: `Runner.catalog()` lived in the deleted file but is still used by non-routing runtime code, so I’ll move that helper next to the catalog type before removing the old router.

The obsolete implementation and its direct test file are gone. I’ve also removed the remaining DDx test references to those private routing helpers and scrubbed stale symbol names from the design note so the acceptance grep is meaningful outside DDx execution artifacts.

The targeted grep over `cli` and `docs` is now clean, and both deleted files are absent. I’m running the Go package tests next because deleting a whole file can expose compile-time dependencies that search alone won’t catch.

The first test run exposed one shared fixture that had lived in the deleted test file: `quotaTestHarness` is still used by `routing_metrics_test.go`, which is not part of the deleted upstream routing suite. I’m moving just that small fixture into the remaining metrics test file rather than restoring routing tests.
`go test ./internal/agent/` is still running after a minute with no output. I’m letting it finish rather than interrupting, since this package has several integration-style tests.

The agent package passes now. I’m doing one broader Go compile/test pass from `cli` as a final guard because deleting exported functions can break packages outside the one we touched even when the direct grep is clean.

The full `go test ./...` failed only in `cmd` installation acceptance tests because `cli/build/ddx` was missing; all non-installation packages, including `internal/agent`, passed. I’m building the expected test binary and rerunning the full suite to separate environment setup from actual code failure.

Verification is clean for the scoped files: no whitespace errors, deleted files absent, symbol grep clean, and `go test ./...` passes after building the binary expected by the installation acceptance tests. I’m staging only the files changed for this bead, leaving the pre-existing `.agents/skills` worktree changes untouched.

The commit hook failed before committing because its race test target requires cgo and the current environment had cgo disabled. I’m rerunning the commit with `CGO_ENABLED=1` so the project hook can execute as intended.
The pre-commit hook is still running; it has completed lint/build output and is now on the slower test portion. I’ll wait for the hook to finish before touching anything else.

Committed bead work:

`00481155 refactor: remove legacy agent routing planner [ddx-ea7f041c]`

Verified:
- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go` deleted
- `cli/internal/agent/routing_test.go` deleted
- No remaining obsolete routing helper references in `cli` or `docs`
- `go test ./internal/agent/` passed
- `go test ./...` passed after building the expected `cli/build/ddx` test binary
- Pre-commit hook passed with `CGO_ENABLED=1`

Unrelated pre-existing `.agents/skills` worktree changes were left untouched.
